title: capparis tenera var. zeylanica
intro: Capparis tenera var. zeylanica, commonly known as the Ceylon caper, is a type of plant native to Sri Lanka, belonging to the Capparaceae family. It is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Ceylon caper include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function, making it a valuable natural remedy for various ailments. Ceylon caper can be prepared as a herbal tea, powder, or oil, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat conditions such as arthritis, ulcers, and respiratory issues.
